[[File:Itakura Raiden Shrine Honsha 1.JPG|thumb|220px|Raiden Shrine (群馬県板倉町)]] '''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社)
'''Raiden Shrine''' (らいでんじんじゃ) is a name shared by multiple [[Shinto shrine]]s located primarily in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. These shrines are commonly dedicated to deities associated with protection against [[thunder]], although their enshrined deities and origins vary.
Similar shrines include {{ill|Raijin Shrine|ja|雷神社}} and {{ill|Den Shrine|ja|電神社}}.
== Gunma Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)|ja|雷電神社 (板倉町)}} – Located in {{ill|Itakura, Gunma|ja|板倉町}}, [[Ōra District]], this shrine is also called Itakura Raiden Shrine. It holds an unofficial status as the head shrine of "Raiden Shrines" in the Kantō region. Enshrined deities include {{ill|Honoikazuchi-no-Ōkami|ja|火雷大神}}, {{ill|Ōikazuchi-no-Ōkami|ja|大雷大神}}, and {{ill|Wakazuchi-no-Ōkami|ja|別雷大神}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Minami, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市南町)}} – Located in [[Minami, Maebashi]] within {{ill|Maebashi, Gunma|ja|前橋市}}, behind the main hall of {{ill|Maebashi Shinmeigu|ja|前橋神明宮}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Kamishinden, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市上新田町)}} – Located in {{ill|Kamishinden, Maebashi|ja|上新田町 (前橋市)}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Heiwa, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市平和町)}} – Located in {{ill|Heiwa, Maebashi|ja|平和町 (前橋市)}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Yokosawa, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市横沢町)}} – Located in Yokosawa, Maebashi. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Taguchi, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市田口町)}} – Located in Taguchi, Maebashi. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Kiryū)|ja|雷電神社 (桐生市)}} – Located in {{ill|Kiryū, Gunma|ja|桐生市}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Isezaki, Sakae-Iyoku)|ja|雷電神社 (伊勢崎市境伊与久)}} – Located in {{ill|Sakae, Iyoku|ja|境伊与久}}, {{ill|Isezaki, Gunma|ja|伊勢崎市}}.
== Tochigi Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Hiraide, Utsunomiya)|ja|雷電神社 (宇都宮市平出町)}} – Located in {{ill|Hiraide, Utsunomiya|ja|平出町 (宇都宮市)}}, this shrine is also called Hiraide Raiden Shrine. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Wakazuchi-no-Ōkami|ja|別雷之大神}} and nine other deities. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Hosoya, Utsunomiya)|ja|雷電神社 (宇都宮市細谷町)}} – Located in {{ill|Hosoya, Utsunomiya|ja|細谷町 (宇都宮市)}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Sukedo, Ashikaga)|ja|雷電神社 (足利市助戸東山町)}} – Located in {{ill|Sukedo, Ashikaga|ja|助戸東山町}}, {{ill|Ashikaga, Tochigi|ja|足利市}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Nogi, Tochigi)|ja|雷電神社 (野木町)}} – Located in {{ill|Nogi, Tochigi|ja|野木町}}, originally part of {{ill|Yanaka Village|ja|谷中村}}.
== Saitama Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Kaminomura Shrine|ja|上之村神社}} (Daiden Shrine) – Located in {{ill|Kumagaya, Saitama|ja|熊谷市}}, the shrine dates back 1,000 years and is associated with the {{ill|Raijin Shrine (Hyōgo)|ja|雷神社 (豊岡市)}} in {{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|ja|豊岡市}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Satte)|ja|雷電神社 (幸手市)}} – Located in {{ill|Satte, Saitama|ja|幸手市}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Kasukabe)|ja|雷電神社 (春日部市)}} – Located in {{ill|Kasukabe, Saitama|ja|春日部市}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Saitama City)|ja|雷電神社 (さいたま市)}} – Located in {{ill|Imawa, Kita-ku|ja|今羽町}}, {{ill|Saitama City|ja|さいたま市}}. * Raiden Shrine (Tokigawa) – Located in {{ill|Tokigawa, Saitama|ja|ときがわ町}}, atop {{ill|Raiden Mountain (Saitama)|ja|雷電山 (埼玉県)}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Kazo)|ja|雷電神社 (加須市南大桑)}} – Located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}.
== Tokyo ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Shinjuku)|ja|雷電神社 (新宿区)}} – Located at {{ill|Hanazono Shrine|ja|花園神社}} in {{ill|Shinjuku, Tokyo|ja|新宿区}}. Merged with Hanazono Shrine in 1928 but still maintains its presence as {{ill|Raiden Inari Shrine|ja|雷電稲荷神社}}.
== Kanagawa Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Isehara)|ja|雷電神社 (伊勢原市)}} – Located in {{ill|Isehara, Kanagawa|ja|伊勢原市}}, affiliated with {{ill|Hibita Shrine|ja|比々多神社}}.
== Aichi Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Tahara)|ja|雷電神社 (田原市)}} – Located in {{ill|Tahara, Aichi|ja|田原市}}.
== Mie Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Ise)|ja|雷電神社 (伊勢市)}} – Located in {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}. Considered a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, though later merged with the Hachioji Shrine.
== Related Articles ==

'''Raiden Shrine''' (らいでんじんじゃ) is a name shared by multiple [[Shinto shrine]]s located primarily in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. These shrines are commonly dedicated to deities associated with protection against [[thunder]], although their enshrined deities and origins vary.
Similar shrines include {{ill|Raijin Shrine|ja|雷神社}} and {{ill|Den Shrine|ja|電神社}}.

Enshrined deities include {{ill|Honoikazuchi-no-Ōkami|ja|火雷大神}}, {{ill|Ōikazuchi-no-Ōkami|ja|大雷大神}}, and {{ill|Wakazuchi-no-Ōkami|ja|別雷大神}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Minami, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市南町)}} – Located in [[Minami, Maebashi]] within {{ill|Maebashi, Gunma|ja|前橋市}}, behind the main hall of {{ill|Maebashi Shinmeigu|ja|前橋神明宮}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Kamishinden, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市上新田町)}} – Located in {{ill|Kamishinden, Maebashi|ja|上新田町 (前橋市)}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Heiwa, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市平和町)}} – Located in {{ill|Heiwa, Maebashi|ja|平和町 (前橋市)}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Yokosawa, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市横沢町)}} – Located in Yokosawa, Maebashi. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Taguchi, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市田口町)}} – Located in Taguchi, Maebashi. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Kiryū)|ja|雷電神社 (桐生市)}} – Located in {{ill|Kiryū, Gunma|ja|桐生市}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Isezaki, Sakae-Iyoku)|ja|雷電神社 (伊勢崎市境伊与久)}} – Located in {{ill|Sakae, Iyoku|ja|境伊与久}}, {{ill|Isezaki, Gunma|ja|伊勢崎市}}.

'''Raiden Shrine''' (らいでんじんじゃ) is a name shared by multiple [[Shinto shrine]]s located primarily in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. These shrines are commonly dedicated to deities associated with protection against [[thunder]], although their enshrined deities and origins vary.
Similar shrines include {{ill|Raijin Shrine|ja|雷神社}} and {{ill|Den Shrine|ja|電神社}}.

[[File:Itakura Raiden Shrine Honsha 1.JPG|thumb|220px|Raiden Shrine (群馬県板倉町)]] '''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社)
'''Raiden Shrine''' (らいでんじんじゃ) is a name shared by multiple [[Shinto shrine]]s located primarily in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. These shrines are commonly dedicated to deities associated with protection against [[thunder]], although their enshrined deities and origins vary.
Similar shrines include {{ill|Raijin Shrine|ja|雷神社}} and {{ill|Den Shrine|ja|電神社}}.
== Gunma Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)|ja|雷電神社 (板倉町)}} – Located in {{ill|Itakura, Gunma|ja|板倉町}}, [[Ōra District]], this shrine is also called Itakura Raiden Shrine. It holds an unofficial status as the head shrine of "Raiden Shrines" in the Kantō region. Enshrined deities include {{ill|Honoikazuchi-no-Ōkami|ja|火雷大神}}, {{ill|Ōikazuchi-no-Ōkami|ja|大雷大神}}, and {{ill|Wakazuchi-no-Ōkami|ja|別雷大神}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Minami, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市南町)}} – Located in [[Minami, Maebashi]] within {{ill|Maebashi, Gunma|ja|前橋市}}, behind the main hall of {{ill|Maebashi Shinmeigu|ja|前橋神明宮}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Kamishinden, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市上新田町)}} – Located in {{ill|Kamishinden, Maebashi|ja|上新田町 (前橋市)}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Heiwa, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市平和町)}} – Located in {{ill|Heiwa, Maebashi|ja|平和町 (前橋市)}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Yokosawa, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市横沢町)}} – Located in Yokosawa, Maebashi. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Taguchi, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市田口町)}} – Located in Taguchi, Maebashi. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Kiryū)|ja|雷電神社 (桐生市)}} – Located in {{ill|Kiryū, Gunma|ja|桐生市}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Isezaki, Sakae-Iyoku)|ja|雷電神社 (伊勢崎市境伊与久)}} – Located in {{ill|Sakae, Iyoku|ja|境伊与久}}, {{ill|Isezaki, Gunma|ja|伊勢崎市}}.

* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Hiraide, Utsunomiya)|ja|雷電神社 (宇都宮市平出町)}} – Located in {{ill|Hiraide, Utsunomiya|ja|平出町 (宇都宮市)}}, this shrine is also called Hiraide Raiden Shrine. '''Raiden Shrine''' (らいでんじんじゃ) is a name shared by multiple [[Shinto shrine]]s located primarily in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. These shrines are commonly dedicated to deities associated with protection against [[thunder]], although their enshrined deities and origins vary.
Similar shrines include {{ill|Raijin Shrine|ja|雷神社}} and {{ill|Den Shrine|ja|電神社}}.

It holds an unofficial status as the head shrine of "Raiden Shrines" in the Kantō region. [[File:Itakura Raiden Shrine Honsha 1.JPG|thumb|220px|Raiden Shrine (群馬県板倉町)]] '''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社)
'''Raiden Shrine''' (らいでんじんじゃ) is a name shared by multiple [[Shinto shrine]]s located primarily in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. These shrines are commonly dedicated to deities associated with protection against [[thunder]], although their enshrined deities and origins vary.
Similar shrines include {{ill|Raijin Shrine|ja|雷神社}} and {{ill|Den Shrine|ja|電神社}}.
== Gunma Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)|ja|雷電神社 (板倉町)}} – Located in {{ill|Itakura, Gunma|ja|板倉町}}, [[Ōra District]], this shrine is also called Itakura Raiden Shrine. It holds an unofficial status as the head shrine of "Raiden Shrines" in the Kantō region. Enshrined deities include {{ill|Honoikazuchi-no-Ōkami|ja|火雷大神}}, {{ill|Ōikazuchi-no-Ōkami|ja|大雷大神}}, and {{ill|Wakazuchi-no-Ōkami|ja|別雷大神}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Minami, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市南町)}} – Located in [[Minami, Maebashi]] within {{ill|Maebashi, Gunma|ja|前橋市}}, behind the main hall of {{ill|Maebashi Shinmeigu|ja|前橋神明宮}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Kamishinden, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市上新田町)}} – Located in {{ill|Kamishinden, Maebashi|ja|上新田町 (前橋市)}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Heiwa, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市平和町)}} – Located in {{ill|Heiwa, Maebashi|ja|平和町 (前橋市)}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Yokosawa, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市横沢町)}} – Located in Yokosawa, Maebashi. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Taguchi, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市田口町)}} – Located in Taguchi, Maebashi. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Kiryū)|ja|雷電神社 (桐生市)}} – Located in {{ill|Kiryū, Gunma|ja|桐生市}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Isezaki, Sakae-Iyoku)|ja|雷電神社 (伊勢崎市境伊与久)}} – Located in {{ill|Sakae, Iyoku|ja|境伊与久}}, {{ill|Isezaki, Gunma|ja|伊勢崎市}}.
== Tochigi Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Hiraide, Utsunomiya)|ja|雷電神社 (宇都宮市平出町)}} – Located in {{ill|Hiraide, Utsunomiya|ja|平出町 (宇都宮市)}}, this shrine is also called Hiraide Raiden Shrine. == Saitama Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Kaminomura Shrine|ja|上之村神社}} (Daiden Shrine) – Located in {{ill|Kumagaya, Saitama|ja|熊谷市}}, the shrine dates back 1,000 years and is associated with the {{ill|Raijin Shrine (Hyōgo)|ja|雷神社 (豊岡市)}} in {{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|ja|豊岡市}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Satte)|ja|雷電神社 (幸手市)}} – Located in {{ill|Satte, Saitama|ja|幸手市}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Kasukabe)|ja|雷電神社 (春日部市)}} – Located in {{ill|Kasukabe, Saitama|ja|春日部市}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Saitama City)|ja|雷電神社 (さいたま市)}} – Located in {{ill|Imawa, Kita-ku|ja|今羽町}}, {{ill|Saitama City|ja|さいたま市}}. * Raiden Shrine (Tokigawa) – Located in {{ill|Tokigawa, Saitama|ja|ときがわ町}}, atop {{ill|Raiden Mountain (Saitama)|ja|雷電山 (埼玉県)}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Kazo)|ja|雷電神社 (加須市南大桑)}} – Located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}.
== Tokyo ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Shinjuku)|ja|雷電神社 (新宿区)}} – Located at {{ill|Hanazono Shrine|ja|花園神社}} in {{ill|Shinjuku, Tokyo|ja|新宿区}}. Merged with Hanazono Shrine in 1928 but still maintains its presence as {{ill|Raiden Inari Shrine|ja|雷電稲荷神社}}.
== Kanagawa Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Isehara)|ja|雷電神社 (伊勢原市)}} – Located in {{ill|Isehara, Kanagawa|ja|伊勢原市}}, affiliated with {{ill|Hibita Shrine|ja|比々多神社}}.

[[File:Itakura Raiden Shrine Honsha 1.JPG|thumb|220px|Raiden Shrine (群馬県板倉町)]] '''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社)
'''Raiden Shrine''' (らいでんじんじゃ) is a name shared by multiple [[Shinto shrine]]s located primarily in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. These shrines are commonly dedicated to deities associated with protection against [[thunder]], although their enshrined deities and origins vary.
Similar shrines include {{ill|Raijin Shrine|ja|雷神社}} and {{ill|Den Shrine|ja|電神社}}.
== Gunma Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)|ja|雷電神社 (板倉町)}} – Located in {{ill|Itakura, Gunma|ja|板倉町}}, [[Ōra District]], this shrine is also called Itakura Raiden Shrine. It holds an unofficial status as the head shrine of "Raiden Shrines" in the Kantō region. Enshrined deities include {{ill|Honoikazuchi-no-Ōkami|ja|火雷大神}}, {{ill|Ōikazuchi-no-Ōkami|ja|大雷大神}}, and {{ill|Wakazuchi-no-Ōkami|ja|別雷大神}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Minami, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市南町)}} – Located in [[Minami, Maebashi]] within {{ill|Maebashi, Gunma|ja|前橋市}}, behind the main hall of {{ill|Maebashi Shinmeigu|ja|前橋神明宮}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Kamishinden, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市上新田町)}} – Located in {{ill|Kamishinden, Maebashi|ja|上新田町 (前橋市)}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Heiwa, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市平和町)}} – Located in {{ill|Heiwa, Maebashi|ja|平和町 (前橋市)}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Yokosawa, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市横沢町)}} – Located in Yokosawa, Maebashi. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Taguchi, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市田口町)}} – Located in Taguchi, Maebashi. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Kiryū)|ja|雷電神社 (桐生市)}} – Located in {{ill|Kiryū, Gunma|ja|桐生市}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Isezaki, Sakae-Iyoku)|ja|雷電神社 (伊勢崎市境伊与久)}} – Located in {{ill|Sakae, Iyoku|ja|境伊与久}}, {{ill|Isezaki, Gunma|ja|伊勢崎市}}.
== Tochigi Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Hiraide, Utsunomiya)|ja|雷電神社 (宇都宮市平出町)}} – Located in {{ill|Hiraide, Utsunomiya|ja|平出町 (宇都宮市)}}, this shrine is also called Hiraide Raiden Shrine. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Wakazuchi-no-Ōkami|ja|別雷之大神}} and nine other deities. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Hosoya, Utsunomiya)|ja|雷電神社 (宇都宮市細谷町)}} – Located in {{ill|Hosoya, Utsunomiya|ja|細谷町 (宇都宮市)}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Sukedo, Ashikaga)|ja|雷電神社 (足利市助戸東山町)}} – Located in {{ill|Sukedo, Ashikaga|ja|助戸東山町}}, {{ill|Ashikaga, Tochigi|ja|足利市}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Nogi, Tochigi)|ja|雷電神社 (野木町)}} – Located in {{ill|Nogi, Tochigi|ja|野木町}}, originally part of {{ill|Yanaka Village|ja|谷中村}}.
== Saitama Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Kaminomura Shrine|ja|上之村神社}} (Daiden Shrine) – Located in {{ill|Kumagaya, Saitama|ja|熊谷市}}, the shrine dates back 1,000 years and is associated with the {{ill|Raijin Shrine (Hyōgo)|ja|雷神社 (豊岡市)}} in {{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|ja|豊岡市}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Satte)|ja|雷電神社 (幸手市)}} – Located in {{ill|Satte, Saitama|ja|幸手市}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Kasukabe)|ja|雷電神社 (春日部市)}} – Located in {{ill|Kasukabe, Saitama|ja|春日部市}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Saitama City)|ja|雷電神社 (さいたま市)}} – Located in {{ill|Imawa, Kita-ku|ja|今羽町}}, {{ill|Saitama City|ja|さいたま市}}. * Raiden Shrine (Tokigawa) – Located in {{ill|Tokigawa, Saitama|ja|ときがわ町}}, atop {{ill|Raiden Mountain (Saitama)|ja|雷電山 (埼玉県)}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Kazo)|ja|雷電神社 (加須市南大桑)}} – Located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}.
== Tokyo ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Shinjuku)|ja|雷電神社 (新宿区)}} – Located at {{ill|Hanazono Shrine|ja|花園神社}} in {{ill|Shinjuku, Tokyo|ja|新宿区}}. Merged with Hanazono Shrine in 1928 but still maintains its presence as {{ill|Raiden Inari Shrine|ja|雷電稲荷神社}}.
== Kanagawa Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Isehara)|ja|雷電神社 (伊勢原市)}} – Located in {{ill|Isehara, Kanagawa|ja|伊勢原市}}, affiliated with {{ill|Hibita Shrine|ja|比々多神社}}.
== Aichi Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Tahara)|ja|雷電神社 (田原市)}} – Located in {{ill|Tahara, Aichi|ja|田原市}}.
== Mie Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Ise)|ja|雷電神社 (伊勢市)}} – Located in {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}. Considered a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, though later merged with the Hachioji Shrine.
== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|Raijin Shrine|ja|雷神社}} * {{ill|Den Shrine|ja|電神社}} * {{ill|Wakaden Shrine|ja|若電神社}} * {{ill|Lightning|ja|落雷}} * {{ill|Thunder|ja|雷}}

'''Raiden Shrine''' (らいでんじんじゃ) is a name shared by multiple [[Shinto shrine]]s located primarily in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. These shrines are commonly dedicated to deities associated with protection against [[thunder]], although their enshrined deities and origins vary.
Similar shrines include {{ill|Raijin Shrine|ja|雷神社}} and {{ill|Den Shrine|ja|電神社}}.
== Gunma Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)|ja|雷電神社 (板倉町)}} – Located in {{ill|Itakura, Gunma|ja|板倉町}}, [[Ōra District]], this shrine is also called Itakura Raiden Shrine. It holds an unofficial status as the head shrine of "Raiden Shrines" in the Kantō region. Enshrined deities include {{ill|Honoikazuchi-no-Ōkami|ja|火雷大神}}, {{ill|Ōikazuchi-no-Ōkami|ja|大雷大神}}, and {{ill|Wakazuchi-no-Ōkami|ja|別雷大神}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Minami, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市南町)}} – Located in [[Minami, Maebashi]] within {{ill|Maebashi, Gunma|ja|前橋市}}, behind the main hall of {{ill|Maebashi Shinmeigu|ja|前橋神明宮}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Kamishinden, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市上新田町)}} – Located in {{ill|Kamishinden, Maebashi|ja|上新田町 (前橋市)}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Heiwa, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市平和町)}} – Located in {{ill|Heiwa, Maebashi|ja|平和町 (前橋市)}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Yokosawa, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市横沢町)}} – Located in Yokosawa, Maebashi. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Taguchi, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市田口町)}} – Located in Taguchi, Maebashi. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Kiryū)|ja|雷電神社 (桐生市)}} – Located in {{ill|Kiryū, Gunma|ja|桐生市}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Isezaki, Sakae-Iyoku)|ja|雷電神社 (伊勢崎市境伊与久)}} – Located in {{ill|Sakae, Iyoku|ja|境伊与久}}, {{ill|Isezaki, Gunma|ja|伊勢崎市}}.

[[File:Itakura Raiden Shrine Honsha 1.JPG|thumb|220px|Raiden Shrine (群馬県板倉町)]] '''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社)
'''Raiden Shrine''' (らいでんじんじゃ) is a name shared by multiple [[Shinto shrine]]s located primarily in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. These shrines are commonly dedicated to deities associated with protection against [[thunder]], although their enshrined deities and origins vary.
Similar shrines include {{ill|Raijin Shrine|ja|雷神社}} and {{ill|Den Shrine|ja|電神社}}.
== Gunma Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)|ja|雷電神社 (板倉町)}} – Located in {{ill|Itakura, Gunma|ja|板倉町}}, [[Ōra District]], this shrine is also called Itakura Raiden Shrine. It holds an unofficial status as the head shrine of "Raiden Shrines" in the Kantō region. Enshrined deities include {{ill|Honoikazuchi-no-Ōkami|ja|火雷大神}}, {{ill|Ōikazuchi-no-Ōkami|ja|大雷大神}}, and {{ill|Wakazuchi-no-Ōkami|ja|別雷大神}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Minami, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市南町)}} – Located in [[Minami, Maebashi]] within {{ill|Maebashi, Gunma|ja|前橋市}}, behind the main hall of {{ill|Maebashi Shinmeigu|ja|前橋神明宮}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Kamishinden, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市上新田町)}} – Located in {{ill|Kamishinden, Maebashi|ja|上新田町 (前橋市)}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Heiwa, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市平和町)}} – Located in {{ill|Heiwa, Maebashi|ja|平和町 (前橋市)}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Yokosawa, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市横沢町)}} – Located in Yokosawa, Maebashi. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Taguchi, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市田口町)}} – Located in Taguchi, Maebashi. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Kiryū)|ja|雷電神社 (桐生市)}} – Located in {{ill|Kiryū, Gunma|ja|桐生市}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Isezaki, Sakae-Iyoku)|ja|雷電神社 (伊勢崎市境伊与久)}} – Located in {{ill|Sakae, Iyoku|ja|境伊与久}}, {{ill|Isezaki, Gunma|ja|伊勢崎市}}.
== Tochigi Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Hiraide, Utsunomiya)|ja|雷電神社 (宇都宮市平出町)}} – Located in {{ill|Hiraide, Utsunomiya|ja|平出町 (宇都宮市)}}, this shrine is also called Hiraide Raiden Shrine. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Wakazuchi-no-Ōkami|ja|別雷之大神}} and nine other deities. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Hosoya, Utsunomiya)|ja|雷電神社 (宇都宮市細谷町)}} – Located in {{ill|Hosoya, Utsunomiya|ja|細谷町 (宇都宮市)}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Sukedo, Ashikaga)|ja|雷電神社 (足利市助戸東山町)}} – Located in {{ill|Sukedo, Ashikaga|ja|助戸東山町}}, {{ill|Ashikaga, Tochigi|ja|足利市}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Nogi, Tochigi)|ja|雷電神社 (野木町)}} – Located in {{ill|Nogi, Tochigi|ja|野木町}}, originally part of {{ill|Yanaka Village|ja|谷中村}}.
== Saitama Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Kaminomura Shrine|ja|上之村神社}} (Daiden Shrine) – Located in {{ill|Kumagaya, Saitama|ja|熊谷市}}, the shrine dates back 1,000 years and is associated with the {{ill|Raijin Shrine (Hyōgo)|ja|雷神社 (豊岡市)}} in {{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|ja|豊岡市}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Satte)|ja|雷電神社 (幸手市)}} – Located in {{ill|Satte, Saitama|ja|幸手市}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Kasukabe)|ja|雷電神社 (春日部市)}} – Located in {{ill|Kasukabe, Saitama|ja|春日部市}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Saitama City)|ja|雷電神社 (さいたま市)}} – Located in {{ill|Imawa, Kita-ku|ja|今羽町}}, {{ill|Saitama City|ja|さいたま市}}. * Raiden Shrine (Tokigawa) – Located in {{ill|Tokigawa, Saitama|ja|ときがわ町}}, atop {{ill|Raiden Mountain (Saitama)|ja|雷電山 (埼玉県)}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Kazo)|ja|雷電神社 (加須市南大桑)}} – Located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}.
== Tokyo ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Shinjuku)|ja|雷電神社 (新宿区)}} – Located at {{ill|Hanazono Shrine|ja|花園神社}} in {{ill|Shinjuku, Tokyo|ja|新宿区}}. Merged with Hanazono Shrine in 1928 but still maintains its presence as {{ill|Raiden Inari Shrine|ja|雷電稲荷神社}}.
== Kanagawa Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Isehara)|ja|雷電神社 (伊勢原市)}} – Located in {{ill|Isehara, Kanagawa|ja|伊勢原市}}, affiliated with {{ill|Hibita Shrine|ja|比々多神社}}.
== Aichi Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Tahara)|ja|雷電神社 (田原市)}} – Located in {{ill|Tahara, Aichi|ja|田原市}}.
== Mie Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Ise)|ja|雷電神社 (伊勢市)}} – Located in {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}. Considered a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, though later merged with the Hachioji Shrine.
== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|Raijin Shrine|ja|雷神社}} * {{ill|Den Shrine|ja|電神社}} * {{ill|Wakaden Shrine|ja|若電神社}} * {{ill|Lightning|ja|落雷}} * {{ill|Thunder|ja|雷}}

The shrine enshrines {{ill|Wakazuchi-no-Ōkami|ja|別雷之大神}} and nine other deities. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Hosoya, Utsunomiya)|ja|雷電神社 (宇都宮市細谷町)}} – Located in {{ill|Hosoya, Utsunomiya|ja|細谷町 (宇都宮市)}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Sukedo, Ashikaga)|ja|雷電神社 (足利市助戸東山町)}} – Located in {{ill|Sukedo, Ashikaga|ja|助戸東山町}}, {{ill|Ashikaga, Tochigi|ja|足利市}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Nogi, Tochigi)|ja|雷電神社 (野木町)}} – Located in {{ill|Nogi, Tochigi|ja|野木町}}, originally part of {{ill|Yanaka Village|ja|谷中村}}.

* {{ill|Kaminomura Shrine|ja|上之村神社}} (Daiden Shrine) – Located in {{ill|Kumagaya, Saitama|ja|熊谷市}}, the shrine dates back 1,000 years and is associated with the {{ill|Raijin Shrine (Hyōgo)|ja|雷神社 (豊岡市)}} in {{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|ja|豊岡市}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Satte)|ja|雷電神社 (幸手市)}} – Located in {{ill|Satte, Saitama|ja|幸手市}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Kasukabe)|ja|雷電神社 (春日部市)}} – Located in {{ill|Kasukabe, Saitama|ja|春日部市}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Saitama City)|ja|雷電神社 (さいたま市)}} – Located in {{ill|Imawa, Kita-ku|ja|今羽町}}, {{ill|Saitama City|ja|さいたま市}}. * Raiden Shrine (Tokigawa) – Located in {{ill|Tokigawa, Saitama|ja|ときがわ町}}, atop {{ill|Raiden Mountain (Saitama)|ja|雷電山 (埼玉県)}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Kazo)|ja|雷電神社 (加須市南大桑)}} – Located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}.

[[File:Itakura Raiden Shrine Honsha 1.JPG|thumb|220px|Raiden Shrine (群馬県板倉町)]] '''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社)
'''Raiden Shrine''' (らいでんじんじゃ) is a name shared by multiple [[Shinto shrine]]s located primarily in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. These shrines are commonly dedicated to deities associated with protection against [[thunder]], although their enshrined deities and origins vary.
Similar shrines include {{ill|Raijin Shrine|ja|雷神社}} and {{ill|Den Shrine|ja|電神社}}.
== Gunma Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)|ja|雷電神社 (板倉町)}} – Located in {{ill|Itakura, Gunma|ja|板倉町}}, [[Ōra District]], this shrine is also called Itakura Raiden Shrine. It holds an unofficial status as the head shrine of "Raiden Shrines" in the Kantō region. Enshrined deities include {{ill|Honoikazuchi-no-Ōkami|ja|火雷大神}}, {{ill|Ōikazuchi-no-Ōkami|ja|大雷大神}}, and {{ill|Wakazuchi-no-Ōkami|ja|別雷大神}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Minami, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市南町)}} – Located in [[Minami, Maebashi]] within {{ill|Maebashi, Gunma|ja|前橋市}}, behind the main hall of {{ill|Maebashi Shinmeigu|ja|前橋神明宮}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Kamishinden, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市上新田町)}} – Located in {{ill|Kamishinden, Maebashi|ja|上新田町 (前橋市)}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Heiwa, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市平和町)}} – Located in {{ill|Heiwa, Maebashi|ja|平和町 (前橋市)}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Yokosawa, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市横沢町)}} – Located in Yokosawa, Maebashi. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Taguchi, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市田口町)}} – Located in Taguchi, Maebashi. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Kiryū)|ja|雷電神社 (桐生市)}} – Located in {{ill|Kiryū, Gunma|ja|桐生市}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Isezaki, Sakae-Iyoku)|ja|雷電神社 (伊勢崎市境伊与久)}} – Located in {{ill|Sakae, Iyoku|ja|境伊与久}}, {{ill|Isezaki, Gunma|ja|伊勢崎市}}.
== Tochigi Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Hiraide, Utsunomiya)|ja|雷電神社 (宇都宮市平出町)}} – Located in {{ill|Hiraide, Utsunomiya|ja|平出町 (宇都宮市)}}, this shrine is also called Hiraide Raiden Shrine. == Kanagawa Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Isehara)|ja|雷電神社 (伊勢原市)}} – Located in {{ill|Isehara, Kanagawa|ja|伊勢原市}}, affiliated with {{ill|Hibita Shrine|ja|比々多神社}}.

[[File:Itakura Raiden Shrine Honsha 1.JPG|thumb|220px|Raiden Shrine (群馬県板倉町)]] '''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社)
'''Raiden Shrine''' (らいでんじんじゃ) is a name shared by multiple [[Shinto shrine]]s located primarily in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. These shrines are commonly dedicated to deities associated with protection against [[thunder]], although their enshrined deities and origins vary.
Similar shrines include {{ill|Raijin Shrine|ja|雷神社}} and {{ill|Den Shrine|ja|電神社}}.
== Gunma Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)|ja|雷電神社 (板倉町)}} – Located in {{ill|Itakura, Gunma|ja|板倉町}}, [[Ōra District]], this shrine is also called Itakura Raiden Shrine. It holds an unofficial status as the head shrine of "Raiden Shrines" in the Kantō region. Enshrined deities include {{ill|Honoikazuchi-no-Ōkami|ja|火雷大神}}, {{ill|Ōikazuchi-no-Ōkami|ja|大雷大神}}, and {{ill|Wakazuchi-no-Ōkami|ja|別雷大神}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Minami, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市南町)}} – Located in [[Minami, Maebashi]] within {{ill|Maebashi, Gunma|ja|前橋市}}, behind the main hall of {{ill|Maebashi Shinmeigu|ja|前橋神明宮}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Kamishinden, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市上新田町)}} – Located in {{ill|Kamishinden, Maebashi|ja|上新田町 (前橋市)}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Heiwa, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市平和町)}} – Located in {{ill|Heiwa, Maebashi|ja|平和町 (前橋市)}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Yokosawa, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市横沢町)}} – Located in Yokosawa, Maebashi. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Taguchi, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市田口町)}} – Located in Taguchi, Maebashi. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Kiryū)|ja|雷電神社 (桐生市)}} – Located in {{ill|Kiryū, Gunma|ja|桐生市}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Isezaki, Sakae-Iyoku)|ja|雷電神社 (伊勢崎市境伊与久)}} – Located in {{ill|Sakae, Iyoku|ja|境伊与久}}, {{ill|Isezaki, Gunma|ja|伊勢崎市}}.
== Tochigi Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Hiraide, Utsunomiya)|ja|雷電神社 (宇都宮市平出町)}} – Located in {{ill|Hiraide, Utsunomiya|ja|平出町 (宇都宮市)}}, this shrine is also called Hiraide Raiden Shrine. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Wakazuchi-no-Ōkami|ja|別雷之大神}} and nine other deities. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Hosoya, Utsunomiya)|ja|雷電神社 (宇都宮市細谷町)}} – Located in {{ill|Hosoya, Utsunomiya|ja|細谷町 (宇都宮市)}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Sukedo, Ashikaga)|ja|雷電神社 (足利市助戸東山町)}} – Located in {{ill|Sukedo, Ashikaga|ja|助戸東山町}}, {{ill|Ashikaga, Tochigi|ja|足利市}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Nogi, Tochigi)|ja|雷電神社 (野木町)}} – Located in {{ill|Nogi, Tochigi|ja|野木町}}, originally part of {{ill|Yanaka Village|ja|谷中村}}.
== Saitama Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Kaminomura Shrine|ja|上之村神社}} (Daiden Shrine) – Located in {{ill|Kumagaya, Saitama|ja|熊谷市}}, the shrine dates back 1,000 years and is associated with the {{ill|Raijin Shrine (Hyōgo)|ja|雷神社 (豊岡市)}} in {{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|ja|豊岡市}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Satte)|ja|雷電神社 (幸手市)}} – Located in {{ill|Satte, Saitama|ja|幸手市}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Kasukabe)|ja|雷電神社 (春日部市)}} – Located in {{ill|Kasukabe, Saitama|ja|春日部市}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Saitama City)|ja|雷電神社 (さいたま市)}} – Located in {{ill|Imawa, Kita-ku|ja|今羽町}}, {{ill|Saitama City|ja|さいたま市}}. * Raiden Shrine (Tokigawa) – Located in {{ill|Tokigawa, Saitama|ja|ときがわ町}}, atop {{ill|Raiden Mountain (Saitama)|ja|雷電山 (埼玉県)}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Kazo)|ja|雷電神社 (加須市南大桑)}} – Located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}.
== Tokyo ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Shinjuku)|ja|雷電神社 (新宿区)}} – Located at {{ill|Hanazono Shrine|ja|花園神社}} in {{ill|Shinjuku, Tokyo|ja|新宿区}}. Merged with Hanazono Shrine in 1928 but still maintains its presence as {{ill|Raiden Inari Shrine|ja|雷電稲荷神社}}.
== Kanagawa Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Isehara)|ja|雷電神社 (伊勢原市)}} – Located in {{ill|Isehara, Kanagawa|ja|伊勢原市}}, affiliated with {{ill|Hibita Shrine|ja|比々多神社}}.
== Aichi Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Tahara)|ja|雷電神社 (田原市)}} – Located in {{ill|Tahara, Aichi|ja|田原市}}.
== Mie Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Ise)|ja|雷電神社 (伊勢市)}} – Located in {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}. Considered a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, though later merged with the Hachioji Shrine.
== Related Articles ==

[[File:Itakura Raiden Shrine Honsha 1.JPG|thumb|220px|Raiden Shrine (群馬県板倉町)]] '''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社)
'''Raiden Shrine''' (らいでんじんじゃ) is a name shared by multiple [[Shinto shrine]]s located primarily in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. These shrines are commonly dedicated to deities associated with protection against [[thunder]], although their enshrined deities and origins vary.
Similar shrines include {{ill|Raijin Shrine|ja|雷神社}} and {{ill|Den Shrine|ja|電神社}}.
== Gunma Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)|ja|雷電神社 (板倉町)}} – Located in {{ill|Itakura, Gunma|ja|板倉町}}, [[Ōra District]], this shrine is also called Itakura Raiden Shrine. It holds an unofficial status as the head shrine of "Raiden Shrines" in the Kantō region. Enshrined deities include {{ill|Honoikazuchi-no-Ōkami|ja|火雷大神}}, {{ill|Ōikazuchi-no-Ōkami|ja|大雷大神}}, and {{ill|Wakazuchi-no-Ōkami|ja|別雷大神}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Minami, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市南町)}} – Located in [[Minami, Maebashi]] within {{ill|Maebashi, Gunma|ja|前橋市}}, behind the main hall of {{ill|Maebashi Shinmeigu|ja|前橋神明宮}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Kamishinden, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市上新田町)}} – Located in {{ill|Kamishinden, Maebashi|ja|上新田町 (前橋市)}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Heiwa, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市平和町)}} – Located in {{ill|Heiwa, Maebashi|ja|平和町 (前橋市)}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Yokosawa, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市横沢町)}} – Located in Yokosawa, Maebashi. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Taguchi, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市田口町)}} – Located in Taguchi, Maebashi. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Kiryū)|ja|雷電神社 (桐生市)}} – Located in {{ill|Kiryū, Gunma|ja|桐生市}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Isezaki, Sakae-Iyoku)|ja|雷電神社 (伊勢崎市境伊与久)}} – Located in {{ill|Sakae, Iyoku|ja|境伊与久}}, {{ill|Isezaki, Gunma|ja|伊勢崎市}}.
== Tochigi Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Hiraide, Utsunomiya)|ja|雷電神社 (宇都宮市平出町)}} – Located in {{ill|Hiraide, Utsunomiya|ja|平出町 (宇都宮市)}}, this shrine is also called Hiraide Raiden Shrine. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Wakazuchi-no-Ōkami|ja|別雷之大神}} and nine other deities. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Hosoya, Utsunomiya)|ja|雷電神社 (宇都宮市細谷町)}} – Located in {{ill|Hosoya, Utsunomiya|ja|細谷町 (宇都宮市)}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Sukedo, Ashikaga)|ja|雷電神社 (足利市助戸東山町)}} – Located in {{ill|Sukedo, Ashikaga|ja|助戸東山町}}, {{ill|Ashikaga, Tochigi|ja|足利市}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Nogi, Tochigi)|ja|雷電神社 (野木町)}} – Located in {{ill|Nogi, Tochigi|ja|野木町}}, originally part of {{ill|Yanaka Village|ja|谷中村}}.
== Saitama Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Kaminomura Shrine|ja|上之村神社}} (Daiden Shrine) – Located in {{ill|Kumagaya, Saitama|ja|熊谷市}}, the shrine dates back 1,000 years and is associated with the {{ill|Raijin Shrine (Hyōgo)|ja|雷神社 (豊岡市)}} in {{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|ja|豊岡市}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Satte)|ja|雷電神社 (幸手市)}} – Located in {{ill|Satte, Saitama|ja|幸手市}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Kasukabe)|ja|雷電神社 (春日部市)}} – Located in {{ill|Kasukabe, Saitama|ja|春日部市}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Saitama City)|ja|雷電神社 (さいたま市)}} – Located in {{ill|Imawa, Kita-ku|ja|今羽町}}, {{ill|Saitama City|ja|さいたま市}}. * Raiden Shrine (Tokigawa) – Located in {{ill|Tokigawa, Saitama|ja|ときがわ町}}, atop {{ill|Raiden Mountain (Saitama)|ja|雷電山 (埼玉県)}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Kazo)|ja|雷電神社 (加須市南大桑)}} – Located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}.
== Tokyo ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Shinjuku)|ja|雷電神社 (新宿区)}} – Located at {{ill|Hanazono Shrine|ja|花園神社}} in {{ill|Shinjuku, Tokyo|ja|新宿区}}. Merged with Hanazono Shrine in 1928 but still maintains its presence as {{ill|Raiden Inari Shrine|ja|雷電稲荷神社}}.
== Kanagawa Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Isehara)|ja|雷電神社 (伊勢原市)}} – Located in {{ill|Isehara, Kanagawa|ja|伊勢原市}}, affiliated with {{ill|Hibita Shrine|ja|比々多神社}}.
== Aichi Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Tahara)|ja|雷電神社 (田原市)}} – Located in {{ill|Tahara, Aichi|ja|田原市}}.
== Mie Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Ise)|ja|雷電神社 (伊勢市)}} – Located in {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}. Considered a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, though later merged with the Hachioji Shrine.
== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|Raijin Shrine|ja|雷神社}} * {{ill|Den Shrine|ja|電神社}} * {{ill|Wakaden Shrine|ja|若電神社}} * {{ill|Lightning|ja|落雷}} * {{ill|Thunder|ja|雷}}

'''Raiden Shrine''' (らいでんじんじゃ) is a name shared by multiple [[Shinto shrine]]s located primarily in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. These shrines are commonly dedicated to deities associated with protection against [[thunder]], although their enshrined deities and origins vary.
Similar shrines include {{ill|Raijin Shrine|ja|雷神社}} and {{ill|Den Shrine|ja|電神社}}.
== Gunma Prefecture ==

It holds an unofficial status as the head shrine of "Raiden Shrines" in the Kantō region. Enshrined deities include {{ill|Honoikazuchi-no-Ōkami|ja|火雷大神}}, {{ill|Ōikazuchi-no-Ōkami|ja|大雷大神}}, and {{ill|Wakazuchi-no-Ōkami|ja|別雷大神}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Minami, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市南町)}} – Located in [[Minami, Maebashi]] within {{ill|Maebashi, Gunma|ja|前橋市}}, behind the main hall of {{ill|Maebashi Shinmeigu|ja|前橋神明宮}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Kamishinden, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市上新田町)}} – Located in {{ill|Kamishinden, Maebashi|ja|上新田町 (前橋市)}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Heiwa, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市平和町)}} – Located in {{ill|Heiwa, Maebashi|ja|平和町 (前橋市)}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Yokosawa, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市横沢町)}} – Located in Yokosawa, Maebashi. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Taguchi, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市田口町)}} – Located in Taguchi, Maebashi. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Kiryū)|ja|雷電神社 (桐生市)}} – Located in {{ill|Kiryū, Gunma|ja|桐生市}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Isezaki, Sakae-Iyoku)|ja|雷電神社 (伊勢崎市境伊与久)}} – Located in {{ill|Sakae, Iyoku|ja|境伊与久}}, {{ill|Isezaki, Gunma|ja|伊勢崎市}}.

These shrines are commonly dedicated to deities associated with protection against [[thunder]], although their enshrined deities and origins vary.
Similar shrines include {{ill|Raijin Shrine|ja|雷神社}} and {{ill|Den Shrine|ja|電神社}}.
== Gunma Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)|ja|雷電神社 (板倉町)}} – Located in {{ill|Itakura, Gunma|ja|板倉町}}, [[Ōra District]], this shrine is also called Itakura Raiden Shrine. It holds an unofficial status as the head shrine of "Raiden Shrines" in the Kantō region. Enshrined deities include {{ill|Honoikazuchi-no-Ōkami|ja|火雷大神}}, {{ill|Ōikazuchi-no-Ōkami|ja|大雷大神}}, and {{ill|Wakazuchi-no-Ōkami|ja|別雷大神}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Minami, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市南町)}} – Located in [[Minami, Maebashi]] within {{ill|Maebashi, Gunma|ja|前橋市}}, behind the main hall of {{ill|Maebashi Shinmeigu|ja|前橋神明宮}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Kamishinden, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市上新田町)}} – Located in {{ill|Kamishinden, Maebashi|ja|上新田町 (前橋市)}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Heiwa, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市平和町)}} – Located in {{ill|Heiwa, Maebashi|ja|平和町 (前橋市)}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Yokosawa, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市横沢町)}} – Located in Yokosawa, Maebashi. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Taguchi, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市田口町)}} – Located in Taguchi, Maebashi. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Kiryū)|ja|雷電神社 (桐生市)}} – Located in {{ill|Kiryū, Gunma|ja|桐生市}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Isezaki, Sakae-Iyoku)|ja|雷電神社 (伊勢崎市境伊与久)}} – Located in {{ill|Sakae, Iyoku|ja|境伊与久}}, {{ill|Isezaki, Gunma|ja|伊勢崎市}}.
== Tochigi Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Hiraide, Utsunomiya)|ja|雷電神社 (宇都宮市平出町)}} – Located in {{ill|Hiraide, Utsunomiya|ja|平出町 (宇都宮市)}}, this shrine is also called Hiraide Raiden Shrine. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Wakazuchi-no-Ōkami|ja|別雷之大神}} and nine other deities. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Hosoya, Utsunomiya)|ja|雷電神社 (宇都宮市細谷町)}} – Located in {{ill|Hosoya, Utsunomiya|ja|細谷町 (宇都宮市)}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Sukedo, Ashikaga)|ja|雷電神社 (足利市助戸東山町)}} – Located in {{ill|Sukedo, Ashikaga|ja|助戸東山町}}, {{ill|Ashikaga, Tochigi|ja|足利市}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Nogi, Tochigi)|ja|雷電神社 (野木町)}} – Located in {{ill|Nogi, Tochigi|ja|野木町}}, originally part of {{ill|Yanaka Village|ja|谷中村}}.
== Saitama Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Kaminomura Shrine|ja|上之村神社}} (Daiden Shrine) – Located in {{ill|Kumagaya, Saitama|ja|熊谷市}}, the shrine dates back 1,000 years and is associated with the {{ill|Raijin Shrine (Hyōgo)|ja|雷神社 (豊岡市)}} in {{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|ja|豊岡市}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Satte)|ja|雷電神社 (幸手市)}} – Located in {{ill|Satte, Saitama|ja|幸手市}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Kasukabe)|ja|雷電神社 (春日部市)}} – Located in {{ill|Kasukabe, Saitama|ja|春日部市}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Saitama City)|ja|雷電神社 (さいたま市)}} – Located in {{ill|Imawa, Kita-ku|ja|今羽町}}, {{ill|Saitama City|ja|さいたま市}}. * Raiden Shrine (Tokigawa) – Located in {{ill|Tokigawa, Saitama|ja|ときがわ町}}, atop {{ill|Raiden Mountain (Saitama)|ja|雷電山 (埼玉県)}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Kazo)|ja|雷電神社 (加須市南大桑)}} – Located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}.
== Tokyo ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Shinjuku)|ja|雷電神社 (新宿区)}} – Located at {{ill|Hanazono Shrine|ja|花園神社}} in {{ill|Shinjuku, Tokyo|ja|新宿区}}. Merged with Hanazono Shrine in 1928 but still maintains its presence as {{ill|Raiden Inari Shrine|ja|雷電稲荷神社}}.
== Kanagawa Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Isehara)|ja|雷電神社 (伊勢原市)}} – Located in {{ill|Isehara, Kanagawa|ja|伊勢原市}}, affiliated with {{ill|Hibita Shrine|ja|比々多神社}}.
== Aichi Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Tahara)|ja|雷電神社 (田原市)}} – Located in {{ill|Tahara, Aichi|ja|田原市}}.
== Mie Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Ise)|ja|雷電神社 (伊勢市)}} – Located in {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}. Considered a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, though later merged with the Hachioji Shrine.

[[File:Itakura Raiden Shrine Honsha 1.JPG|thumb|220px|Raiden Shrine (群馬県板倉町)]] '''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a name shared by multiple [[Shinto shrine]]s located primarily in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. These shrines are commonly dedicated to deities associated with protection against [[thunder]], although their enshrined deities and origins vary.
Similar shrines include {{ill|Raijin Shrine|ja|雷神社}} and {{ill|Den Shrine|ja|電神社}}.
== Gunma Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)|ja|雷電神社 (板倉町)}} – Located in {{ill|Itakura, Gunma|ja|板倉町}}, [[Ōra District]], this shrine is also called Itakura Raiden Shrine. It holds an unofficial status as the head shrine of "Raiden Shrines" in the Kantō region. Enshrined deities include {{ill|Honoikazuchi-no-Ōkami|ja|火雷大神}}, {{ill|Ōikazuchi-no-Ōkami|ja|大雷大神}}, and {{ill|Wakazuchi-no-Ōkami|ja|別雷大神}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Minami, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市南町)}} – Located in [[Minami, Maebashi]] within {{ill|Maebashi, Gunma|ja|前橋市}}, behind the main hall of {{ill|Maebashi Shinmeigu|ja|前橋神明宮}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Kamishinden, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市上新田町)}} – Located in {{ill|Kamishinden, Maebashi|ja|上新田町 (前橋市)}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Heiwa, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市平和町)}} – Located in {{ill|Heiwa, Maebashi|ja|平和町 (前橋市)}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Yokosawa, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市横沢町)}} – Located in Yokosawa, Maebashi. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Taguchi, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市田口町)}} – Located in Taguchi, Maebashi. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Kiryū)|ja|雷電神社 (桐生市)}} – Located in {{ill|Kiryū, Gunma|ja|桐生市}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Isezaki, Sakae-Iyoku)|ja|雷電神社 (伊勢崎市境伊与久)}} – Located in {{ill|Sakae, Iyoku|ja|境伊与久}}, {{ill|Isezaki, Gunma|ja|伊勢崎市}}.
== Tochigi Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Hiraide, Utsunomiya)|ja|雷電神社 (宇都宮市平出町)}} – Located in {{ill|Hiraide, Utsunomiya|ja|平出町 (宇都宮市)}}, this shrine is also called Hiraide Raiden Shrine. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Wakazuchi-no-Ōkami|ja|別雷之大神}} and nine other deities. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Hosoya, Utsunomiya)|ja|雷電神社 (宇都宮市細谷町)}} – Located in {{ill|Hosoya, Utsunomiya|ja|細谷町 (宇都宮市)}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Sukedo, Ashikaga)|ja|雷電神社 (足利市助戸東山町)}} – Located in {{ill|Sukedo, Ashikaga|ja|助戸東山町}}, {{ill|Ashikaga, Tochigi|ja|足利市}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Nogi, Tochigi)|ja|雷電神社 (野木町)}} – Located in {{ill|Nogi, Tochigi|ja|野木町}}, originally part of {{ill|Yanaka Village|ja|谷中村}}.
== Saitama Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Kaminomura Shrine|ja|上之村神社}} (Daiden Shrine) – Located in {{ill|Kumagaya, Saitama|ja|熊谷市}}, the shrine dates back 1,000 years and is associated with the {{ill|Raijin Shrine (Hyōgo)|ja|雷神社 (豊岡市)}} in {{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|ja|豊岡市}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Satte)|ja|雷電神社 (幸手市)}} – Located in {{ill|Satte, Saitama|ja|幸手市}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Kasukabe)|ja|雷電神社 (春日部市)}} – Located in {{ill|Kasukabe, Saitama|ja|春日部市}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Saitama City)|ja|雷電神社 (さいたま市)}} – Located in {{ill|Imawa, Kita-ku|ja|今羽町}}, {{ill|Saitama City|ja|さいたま市}}. * Raiden Shrine (Tokigawa) – Located in {{ill|Tokigawa, Saitama|ja|ときがわ町}}, atop {{ill|Raiden Mountain (Saitama)|ja|雷電山 (埼玉県)}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Kazo)|ja|雷電神社 (加須市南大桑)}} – Located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}.
== Tokyo ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Shinjuku)|ja|雷電神社 (新宿区)}} – Located at {{ill|Hanazono Shrine|ja|花園神社}} in {{ill|Shinjuku, Tokyo|ja|新宿区}}. Merged with Hanazono Shrine in 1928 but still maintains its presence as {{ill|Raiden Inari Shrine|ja|雷電稲荷神社}}.
== Kanagawa Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Isehara)|ja|雷電神社 (伊勢原市)}} – Located in {{ill|Isehara, Kanagawa|ja|伊勢原市}}, affiliated with {{ill|Hibita Shrine|ja|比々多神社}}.
== Aichi Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Tahara)|ja|雷電神社 (田原市)}} – Located in {{ill|Tahara, Aichi|ja|田原市}}.
== Mie Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Ise)|ja|雷電神社 (伊勢市)}} – Located in {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}. Considered a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, though later merged with the Hachioji Shrine.

[[File:Itakura Raiden Shrine Honsha 1.JPG|thumb|220px|Raiden Shrine (群馬県板倉町)]] '''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a name shared by multiple [[Shinto shrine]]s located primarily in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. These shrines are commonly dedicated to deities associated with protection against [[thunder]], although their enshrined deities and origins vary.
Similar shrines include {{ill|Raijin Shrine|ja|雷神社}} and {{ill|Den Shrine|ja|電神社}}.
== Gunma Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)|ja|雷電神社 (板倉町)}} – Located in {{ill|Itakura, Gunma|ja|板倉町}}, [[Ōra District]], this shrine is also called Itakura Raiden Shrine. It holds an unofficial status as the head shrine of "Raiden Shrines" in the Kantō region. Enshrined deities include {{ill|Honoikazuchi-no-Ōkami|ja|火雷大神}}, {{ill|Ōikazuchi-no-Ōkami|ja|大雷大神}}, and {{ill|Wakazuchi-no-Ōkami|ja|別雷大神}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Minami, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市南町)}} – Located in [[Minami, Maebashi]] within {{ill|Maebashi, Gunma|ja|前橋市}}, behind the main hall of {{ill|Maebashi Shinmeigu|ja|前橋神明宮}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Kamishinden, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市上新田町)}} – Located in {{ill|Kamishinden, Maebashi|ja|上新田町 (前橋市)}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Heiwa, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市平和町)}} – Located in {{ill|Heiwa, Maebashi|ja|平和町 (前橋市)}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Yokosawa, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市横沢町)}} – Located in Yokosawa, Maebashi. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Taguchi, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市田口町)}} – Located in Taguchi, Maebashi. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Kiryū)|ja|雷電神社 (桐生市)}} – Located in {{ill|Kiryū, Gunma|ja|桐生市}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Isezaki, Sakae-Iyoku)|ja|雷電神社 (伊勢崎市境伊与久)}} – Located in {{ill|Sakae, Iyoku|ja|境伊与久}}, {{ill|Isezaki, Gunma|ja|伊勢崎市}}.
== Tochigi Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Hiraide, Utsunomiya)|ja|雷電神社 (宇都宮市平出町)}} – Located in {{ill|Hiraide, Utsunomiya|ja|平出町 (宇都宮市)}}, this shrine is also called Hiraide Raiden Shrine. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Wakazuchi-no-Ōkami|ja|別雷之大神}} and nine other deities. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Hosoya, Utsunomiya)|ja|雷電神社 (宇都宮市細谷町)}} – Located in {{ill|Hosoya, Utsunomiya|ja|細谷町 (宇都宮市)}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Sukedo, Ashikaga)|ja|雷電神社 (足利市助戸東山町)}} – Located in {{ill|Sukedo, Ashikaga|ja|助戸東山町}}, {{ill|Ashikaga, Tochigi|ja|足利市}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Nogi, Tochigi)|ja|雷電神社 (野木町)}} – Located in {{ill|Nogi, Tochigi|ja|野木町}}, originally part of {{ill|Yanaka Village|ja|谷中村}}.
== Saitama Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Kaminomura Shrine|ja|上之村神社}} (Daiden Shrine) – Located in {{ill|Kumagaya, Saitama|ja|熊谷市}}, the shrine dates back 1,000 years and is associated with the {{ill|Raijin Shrine (Hyōgo)|ja|雷神社 (豊岡市)}} in {{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|ja|豊岡市}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Satte)|ja|雷電神社 (幸手市)}} – Located in {{ill|Satte, Saitama|ja|幸手市}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Kasukabe)|ja|雷電神社 (春日部市)}} – Located in {{ill|Kasukabe, Saitama|ja|春日部市}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Saitama City)|ja|雷電神社 (さいたま市)}} – Located in {{ill|Imawa, Kita-ku|ja|今羽町}}, {{ill|Saitama City|ja|さいたま市}}. * Raiden Shrine (Tokigawa) – Located in {{ill|Tokigawa, Saitama|ja|ときがわ町}}, atop {{ill|Raiden Mountain (Saitama)|ja|雷電山 (埼玉県)}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Kazo)|ja|雷電神社 (加須市南大桑)}} – Located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}.
== Tokyo ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Shinjuku)|ja|雷電神社 (新宿区)}} – Located at {{ill|Hanazono Shrine|ja|花園神社}} in {{ill|Shinjuku, Tokyo|ja|新宿区}}. Merged with Hanazono Shrine in 1928 but still maintains its presence as {{ill|Raiden Inari Shrine|ja|雷電稲荷神社}}.
== Kanagawa Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Isehara)|ja|雷電神社 (伊勢原市)}} – Located in {{ill|Isehara, Kanagawa|ja|伊勢原市}}, affiliated with {{ill|Hibita Shrine|ja|比々多神社}}.
== Aichi Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Tahara)|ja|雷電神社 (田原市)}} – Located in {{ill|Tahara, Aichi|ja|田原市}}.
== Mie Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Ise)|ja|雷電神社 (伊勢市)}} – Located in {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}. Considered a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, though later merged with the Hachioji Shrine.

[[File:Itakura Raiden Shrine Honsha 1.JPG|thumb|220px|Raiden Shrine (群馬県板倉町)]] '''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a name shared by multiple [[Shinto shrine]]s located primarily in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. These shrines are commonly dedicated to deities associated with protection against [[thunder]], although their enshrined deities and origins vary.
Similar shrines include {{ill|Raijin Shrine|ja|雷神社}} and {{ill|Den Shrine|ja|電神社}}.
== Gunma Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)|ja|雷電神社 (板倉町)}} – Located in {{ill|Itakura, Gunma|ja|板倉町}}, [[Ōra District]], this shrine is also called Itakura Raiden Shrine. It holds an unofficial status as the head shrine of "Raiden Shrines" in the Kantō region. Enshrined deities include {{ill|Honoikazuchi-no-Ōkami|ja|火雷大神}}, {{ill|Ōikazuchi-no-Ōkami|ja|大雷大神}}, and {{ill|Wakazuchi-no-Ōkami|ja|別雷大神}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Minami, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市南町)}} – Located in [[Minami, Maebashi]] within {{ill|Maebashi, Gunma|ja|前橋市}}, behind the main hall of {{ill|Maebashi Shinmeigu|ja|前橋神明宮}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Kamishinden, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市上新田町)}} – Located in {{ill|Kamishinden, Maebashi|ja|上新田町 (前橋市)}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Heiwa, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市平和町)}} – Located in {{ill|Heiwa, Maebashi|ja|平和町 (前橋市)}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Yokosawa, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市横沢町)}} – Located in Yokosawa, Maebashi. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Taguchi, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市田口町)}} – Located in Taguchi, Maebashi. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Kiryū)|ja|雷電神社 (桐生市)}} – Located in {{ill|Kiryū, Gunma|ja|桐生市}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Isezaki, Sakae-Iyoku)|ja|雷電神社 (伊勢崎市境伊与久)}} – Located in {{ill|Sakae, Iyoku|ja|境伊与久}}, {{ill|Isezaki, Gunma|ja|伊勢崎市}}.
== Tochigi Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Hiraide, Utsunomiya)|ja|雷電神社 (宇都宮市平出町)}} – Located in {{ill|Hiraide, Utsunomiya|ja|平出町 (宇都宮市)}}, this shrine is also called Hiraide Raiden Shrine. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Wakazuchi-no-Ōkami|ja|別雷之大神}} and nine other deities. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Hosoya, Utsunomiya)|ja|雷電神社 (宇都宮市細谷町)}} – Located in {{ill|Hosoya, Utsunomiya|ja|細谷町 (宇都宮市)}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Sukedo, Ashikaga)|ja|雷電神社 (足利市助戸東山町)}} – Located in {{ill|Sukedo, Ashikaga|ja|助戸東山町}}, {{ill|Ashikaga, Tochigi|ja|足利市}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Nogi, Tochigi)|ja|雷電神社 (野木町)}} – Located in {{ill|Nogi, Tochigi|ja|野木町}}, originally part of {{ill|Yanaka Village|ja|谷中村}}.
== Saitama Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Kaminomura Shrine|ja|上之村神社}} (Daiden Shrine) – Located in {{ill|Kumagaya, Saitama|ja|熊谷市}}, the shrine dates back 1,000 years and is associated with the {{ill|Raijin Shrine (Hyōgo)|ja|雷神社 (豊岡市)}} in {{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|ja|豊岡市}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Satte)|ja|雷電神社 (幸手市)}} – Located in {{ill|Satte, Saitama|ja|幸手市}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Kasukabe)|ja|雷電神社 (春日部市)}} – Located in {{ill|Kasukabe, Saitama|ja|春日部市}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Saitama City)|ja|雷電神社 (さいたま市)}} – Located in {{ill|Imawa, Kita-ku|ja|今羽町}}, {{ill|Saitama City|ja|さいたま市}}. * Raiden Shrine (Tokigawa) – Located in {{ill|Tokigawa, Saitama|ja|ときがわ町}}, atop {{ill|Raiden Mountain (Saitama)|ja|雷電山 (埼玉県)}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Kazo)|ja|雷電神社 (加須市南大桑)}} – Located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}.
== Tokyo ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Shinjuku)|ja|雷電神社 (新宿区)}} – Located at {{ill|Hanazono Shrine|ja|花園神社}} in {{ill|Shinjuku, Tokyo|ja|新宿区}}. Merged with Hanazono Shrine in 1928 but still maintains its presence as {{ill|Raiden Inari Shrine|ja|雷電稲荷神社}}.
== Kanagawa Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Isehara)|ja|雷電神社 (伊勢原市)}} – Located in {{ill|Isehara, Kanagawa|ja|伊勢原市}}, affiliated with {{ill|Hibita Shrine|ja|比々多神社}}.
== Aichi Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Tahara)|ja|雷電神社 (田原市)}} – Located in {{ill|Tahara, Aichi|ja|田原市}}.
== Mie Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Ise)|ja|雷電神社 (伊勢市)}} – Located in {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}. Considered a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, though later merged with the Hachioji Shrine.

[[File:Itakura Raiden Shrine Honsha 1.JPG|thumb|220px|Raiden Shrine (群馬県板倉町)]] '''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a name shared by multiple [[Shinto shrine]]s located primarily in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. These shrines are commonly dedicated to deities associated with protection against [[thunder]], although their enshrined deities and origins vary.
Similar shrines include {{ill|Raijin Shrine|ja|雷神社}} and {{ill|Den Shrine|ja|電神社}}.
== Gunma Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)|ja|雷電神社 (板倉町)}} – Located in {{ill|Itakura, Gunma|ja|板倉町}}, [[Ōra District]], this shrine is also called Itakura Raiden Shrine. It holds an unofficial status as the head shrine of "Raiden Shrines" in the Kantō region. Enshrined deities include {{ill|Honoikazuchi-no-Ōkami|ja|火雷大神}}, {{ill|Ōikazuchi-no-Ōkami|ja|大雷大神}}, and {{ill|Wakazuchi-no-Ōkami|ja|別雷大神}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Minami, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市南町)}} – Located in [[Minami, Maebashi]] within {{ill|Maebashi, Gunma|ja|前橋市}}, behind the main hall of {{ill|Maebashi Shinmeigu|ja|前橋神明宮}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Kamishinden, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市上新田町)}} – Located in {{ill|Kamishinden, Maebashi|ja|上新田町 (前橋市)}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Heiwa, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市平和町)}} – Located in {{ill|Heiwa, Maebashi|ja|平和町 (前橋市)}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Yokosawa, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市横沢町)}} – Located in Yokosawa, Maebashi. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Taguchi, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市田口町)}} – Located in Taguchi, Maebashi. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Kiryū)|ja|雷電神社 (桐生市)}} – Located in {{ill|Kiryū, Gunma|ja|桐生市}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Isezaki, Sakae-Iyoku)|ja|雷電神社 (伊勢崎市境伊与久)}} – Located in {{ill|Sakae, Iyoku|ja|境伊与久}}, {{ill|Isezaki, Gunma|ja|伊勢崎市}}.
== Tochigi Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Hiraide, Utsunomiya)|ja|雷電神社 (宇都宮市平出町)}} – Located in {{ill|Hiraide, Utsunomiya|ja|平出町 (宇都宮市)}}, this shrine is also called Hiraide Raiden Shrine. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Wakazuchi-no-Ōkami|ja|別雷之大神}} and nine other deities. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Hosoya, Utsunomiya)|ja|雷電神社 (宇都宮市細谷町)}} – Located in {{ill|Hosoya, Utsunomiya|ja|細谷町 (宇都宮市)}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Sukedo, Ashikaga)|ja|雷電神社 (足利市助戸東山町)}} – Located in {{ill|Sukedo, Ashikaga|ja|助戸東山町}}, {{ill|Ashikaga, Tochigi|ja|足利市}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Nogi, Tochigi)|ja|雷電神社 (野木町)}} – Located in {{ill|Nogi, Tochigi|ja|野木町}}, originally part of {{ill|Yanaka Village|ja|谷中村}}.
== Saitama Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Kaminomura Shrine|ja|上之村神社}} (Daiden Shrine) – Located in {{ill|Kumagaya, Saitama|ja|熊谷市}}, the shrine dates back 1,000 years and is associated with the {{ill|Raijin Shrine (Hyōgo)|ja|雷神社 (豊岡市)}} in {{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|ja|豊岡市}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Satte)|ja|雷電神社 (幸手市)}} – Located in {{ill|Satte, Saitama|ja|幸手市}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Kasukabe)|ja|雷電神社 (春日部市)}} – Located in {{ill|Kasukabe, Saitama|ja|春日部市}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Saitama City)|ja|雷電神社 (さいたま市)}} – Located in {{ill|Imawa, Kita-ku|ja|今羽町}}, {{ill|Saitama City|ja|さいたま市}}. * Raiden Shrine (Tokigawa) – Located in {{ill|Tokigawa, Saitama|ja|ときがわ町}}, atop {{ill|Raiden Mountain (Saitama)|ja|雷電山 (埼玉県)}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Kazo)|ja|雷電神社 (加須市南大桑)}} – Located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}.
== Tokyo ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Shinjuku)|ja|雷電神社 (新宿区)}} – Located at {{ill|Hanazono Shrine|ja|花園神社}} in {{ill|Shinjuku, Tokyo|ja|新宿区}}. Merged with Hanazono Shrine in 1928 but still maintains its presence as {{ill|Raiden Inari Shrine|ja|雷電稲荷神社}}.
== Kanagawa Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Isehara)|ja|雷電神社 (伊勢原市)}} – Located in {{ill|Isehara, Kanagawa|ja|伊勢原市}}, affiliated with {{ill|Hibita Shrine|ja|比々多神社}}.
== Aichi Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Tahara)|ja|雷電神社 (田原市)}} – Located in {{ill|Tahara, Aichi|ja|田原市}}.
== Mie Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Ise)|ja|雷電神社 (伊勢市)}} – Located in {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}. Considered a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, though later merged with the Hachioji Shrine.
== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|Raijin Shrine|ja|雷神社}} * {{ill|Den Shrine|ja|電神社}} * {{ill|Wakaden Shrine|ja|若電神社}} * {{ill|Lightning|ja|落雷}} * {{ill|Thunder|ja|雷}}

[[File:Itakura Raiden Shrine Honsha 1.JPG|thumb|220px|Raiden Shrine (群馬県板倉町)]] '''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a name shared by multiple [[Shinto shrine]]s located primarily in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. These shrines are commonly dedicated to deities associated with protection against [[thunder]], although their enshrined deities and origins vary.
Similar shrines include {{ill|Raijin Shrine|ja|雷神社}} and {{ill|Den Shrine|ja|電神社}}.
== Gunma Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)|ja|雷電神社 (板倉町)}} – Located in {{ill|Itakura, Gunma|ja|板倉町}}, [[Ōra District]], this shrine is also called Itakura Raiden Shrine. It holds an unofficial status as the head shrine of "Raiden Shrines" in the Kantō region. Enshrined deities include {{ill|Honoikazuchi-no-Ōkami|ja|火雷大神}}, {{ill|Ōikazuchi-no-Ōkami|ja|大雷大神}}, and {{ill|Wakazuchi-no-Ōkami|ja|別雷大神}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Minami, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市南町)}} – Located in [[Minami, Maebashi]] within {{ill|Maebashi, Gunma|ja|前橋市}}, behind the main hall of {{ill|Maebashi Shinmeigu|ja|前橋神明宮}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Kamishinden, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市上新田町)}} – Located in {{ill|Kamishinden, Maebashi|ja|上新田町 (前橋市)}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Heiwa, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市平和町)}} – Located in {{ill|Heiwa, Maebashi|ja|平和町 (前橋市)}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Yokosawa, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市横沢町)}} – Located in Yokosawa, Maebashi. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Taguchi, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市田口町)}} – Located in Taguchi, Maebashi. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Kiryū)|ja|雷電神社 (桐生市)}} – Located in {{ill|Kiryū, Gunma|ja|桐生市}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Isezaki, Sakae-Iyoku)|ja|雷電神社 (伊勢崎市境伊与久)}} – Located in {{ill|Sakae, Iyoku|ja|境伊与久}}, {{ill|Isezaki, Gunma|ja|伊勢崎市}}.
== Tochigi Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Hiraide, Utsunomiya)|ja|雷電神社 (宇都宮市平出町)}} – Located in {{ill|Hiraide, Utsunomiya|ja|平出町 (宇都宮市)}}, this shrine is also called Hiraide Raiden Shrine. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Wakazuchi-no-Ōkami|ja|別雷之大神}} and nine other deities. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Hosoya, Utsunomiya)|ja|雷電神社 (宇都宮市細谷町)}} – Located in {{ill|Hosoya, Utsunomiya|ja|細谷町 (宇都宮市)}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Sukedo, Ashikaga)|ja|雷電神社 (足利市助戸東山町)}} – Located in {{ill|Sukedo, Ashikaga|ja|助戸東山町}}, {{ill|Ashikaga, Tochigi|ja|足利市}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Nogi, Tochigi)|ja|雷電神社 (野木町)}} – Located in {{ill|Nogi, Tochigi|ja|野木町}}, originally part of {{ill|Yanaka Village|ja|谷中村}}.
== Saitama Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Kaminomura Shrine|ja|上之村神社}} (Daiden Shrine) – Located in {{ill|Kumagaya, Saitama|ja|熊谷市}}, the shrine dates back 1,000 years and is associated with the {{ill|Raijin Shrine (Hyōgo)|ja|雷神社 (豊岡市)}} in {{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|ja|豊岡市}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Satte)|ja|雷電神社 (幸手市)}} – Located in {{ill|Satte, Saitama|ja|幸手市}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Kasukabe)|ja|雷電神社 (春日部市)}} – Located in {{ill|Kasukabe, Saitama|ja|春日部市}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Saitama City)|ja|雷電神社 (さいたま市)}} – Located in {{ill|Imawa, Kita-ku|ja|今羽町}}, {{ill|Saitama City|ja|さいたま市}}. * Raiden Shrine (Tokigawa) – Located in {{ill|Tokigawa, Saitama|ja|ときがわ町}}, atop {{ill|Raiden Mountain (Saitama)|ja|雷電山 (埼玉県)}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Kazo)|ja|雷電神社 (加須市南大桑)}} – Located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}.
== Tokyo ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Shinjuku)|ja|雷電神社 (新宿区)}} – Located at {{ill|Hanazono Shrine|ja|花園神社}} in {{ill|Shinjuku, Tokyo|ja|新宿区}}. Merged with Hanazono Shrine in 1928 but still maintains its presence as {{ill|Raiden Inari Shrine|ja|雷電稲荷神社}}.
== Kanagawa Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Isehara)|ja|雷電神社 (伊勢原市)}} – Located in {{ill|Isehara, Kanagawa|ja|伊勢原市}}, affiliated with {{ill|Hibita Shrine|ja|比々多神社}}.
== Aichi Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Tahara)|ja|雷電神社 (田原市)}} – Located in {{ill|Tahara, Aichi|ja|田原市}}.
== Mie Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Ise)|ja|雷電神社 (伊勢市)}} – Located in {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}. Considered a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, though later merged with the Hachioji Shrine.
== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|Raijin Shrine|ja|雷神社}} * {{ill|Den Shrine|ja|電神社}} * {{ill|Wakaden Shrine|ja|若電神社}} * {{ill|Lightning|ja|落雷}} * {{ill|Thunder|ja|雷}}

[[File:Itakura Raiden Shrine Honsha 1.JPG|thumb|220px|Raiden Shrine (群馬県板倉町)]] '''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a name shared by multiple [[Shinto shrine]]s located primarily in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. These shrines are commonly dedicated to deities associated with protection against [[thunder]], although their enshrined deities and origins vary.
Similar shrines include {{ill|Raijin Shrine|ja|雷神社|12=draft}} and {{ill|Den Shrine|ja|電神社|12=draft}}.
== Gunma Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)|ja|雷電神社 (板倉町)|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Itakura, Gunma|ja|板倉町|12=draft}}, [[Ōra District]], this shrine is also called Itakura Raiden Shrine. It holds an unofficial status as the head shrine of "Raiden Shrines" in the Kantō region. Enshrined deities include {{ill|Honoikazuchi-no-Ōkami|ja|火雷大神|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōikazuchi-no-Ōkami|ja|大雷大神|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Wakazuchi-no-Ōkami|ja|別雷大神|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Minami, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市南町)|12=draft}} – Located in [[Minami, Maebashi]] within {{ill|Maebashi, Gunma|ja|前橋市|12=draft}}, behind the main hall of {{ill|Maebashi Shinmeigu|ja|前橋神明宮|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Kamishinden, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市上新田町)|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Kamishinden, Maebashi|ja|上新田町 (前橋市)|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Heiwa, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市平和町)|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Heiwa, Maebashi|ja|平和町 (前橋市)|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Yokosawa, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市横沢町)|12=draft}} – Located in Yokosawa, Maebashi. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Taguchi, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市田口町)|12=draft}} – Located in Taguchi, Maebashi. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Kiryū)|ja|雷電神社 (桐生市)|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Kiryū, Gunma|ja|桐生市|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Isezaki, Sakae-Iyoku)|ja|雷電神社 (伊勢崎市境伊与久)|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Sakae, Iyoku|ja|境伊与久|12=draft}}, {{ill|Isezaki, Gunma|ja|伊勢崎市|12=draft}}.
== Tochigi Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Hiraide, Utsunomiya)|ja|雷電神社 (宇都宮市平出町)|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Hiraide, Utsunomiya|ja|平出町 (宇都宮市)|12=draft}}, this shrine is also called Hiraide Raiden Shrine. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Wakazuchi-no-Ōkami|ja|別雷之大神|12=draft}} and nine other deities. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Hosoya, Utsunomiya)|ja|雷電神社 (宇都宮市細谷町)|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Hosoya, Utsunomiya|ja|細谷町 (宇都宮市)|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Sukedo, Ashikaga)|ja|雷電神社 (足利市助戸東山町)|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Sukedo, Ashikaga|ja|助戸東山町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ashikaga, Tochigi|ja|足利市|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Nogi, Tochigi)|ja|雷電神社 (野木町)|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Nogi, Tochigi|ja|野木町|12=draft}}, originally part of {{ill|Yanaka Village|ja|谷中村|12=draft}}.
== Saitama Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Kaminomura Shrine|ja|上之村神社|12=draft}} (Daiden Shrine) – Located in {{ill|Kumagaya, Saitama|ja|熊谷市|12=draft}}, the shrine dates back 1,000 years and is associated with the {{ill|Raijin Shrine (Hyōgo)|ja|雷神社 (豊岡市)|12=draft}} in {{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|ja|豊岡市|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Satte)|ja|雷電神社 (幸手市)|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Satte, Saitama|ja|幸手市|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Kasukabe)|ja|雷電神社 (春日部市)|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Kasukabe, Saitama|ja|春日部市|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Saitama City)|ja|雷電神社 (さいたま市)|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Imawa, Kita-ku|ja|今羽町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Saitama City|ja|さいたま市|12=draft}}. * Raiden Shrine (Tokigawa) – Located in {{ill|Tokigawa, Saitama|ja|ときがわ町|12=draft}}, atop {{ill|Raiden Mountain (Saitama)|ja|雷電山 (埼玉県)|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Kazo)|ja|雷電神社 (加須市南大桑)|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市|12=draft}}.
== Tokyo ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Shinjuku)|ja|雷電神社 (新宿区)|12=draft}} – Located at {{ill|Hanazono Shrine|ja|花園神社|12=draft}} in {{ill|Shinjuku, Tokyo|ja|新宿区|12=draft}}. Merged with Hanazono Shrine in 1928 but still maintains its presence as {{ill|Raiden Inari Shrine|ja|雷電稲荷神社|12=draft}}.
== Kanagawa Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Isehara)|ja|雷電神社 (伊勢原市)|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Isehara, Kanagawa|ja|伊勢原市|12=draft}}, affiliated with {{ill|Hibita Shrine|ja|比々多神社|12=draft}}.
== Aichi Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Tahara)|ja|雷電神社 (田原市)|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Tahara, Aichi|ja|田原市|12=draft}}.
== Mie Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Ise)|ja|雷電神社 (伊勢市)|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市|12=draft}}. Considered a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|12=draft}}, though later merged with the Hachioji Shrine.
== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|Raijin Shrine|ja|雷神社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Den Shrine|ja|電神社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Wakaden Shrine|ja|若電神社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Lightning|ja|落雷|12=draft}} * {{ill|Thunder|ja|雷|12=draft}}

[[File:Itakura Raiden Shrine Honsha 1.JPG|thumb|220px|Raiden Shrine (群馬県板倉町)]] '''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a name shared by multiple [[Shinto shrine]]s located primarily in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. These shrines are commonly dedicated to deities associated with protection against [[thunder]], although their enshrined deities and origins vary.
Similar shrines include {{ill|Raijin Shrine|ja|雷神社|12=draft}} and {{ill|Den Shrine|ja|電神社|12=draft}}.

Enshrined deities include {{ill|Honoikazuchi-no-Ōkami|ja|火雷大神|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōikazuchi-no-Ōkami|ja|大雷大神|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Wakazuchi-no-Ōkami|ja|別雷大神|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Minami, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市南町)|12=draft}} – Located in [[Minami, Maebashi]] within {{ill|Maebashi, Gunma|ja|前橋市|12=draft}}, behind the main hall of {{ill|Maebashi Shinmeigu|ja|前橋神明宮|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Kamishinden, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市上新田町)|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Kamishinden, Maebashi|ja|上新田町 (前橋市)|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Heiwa, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市平和町)|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Heiwa, Maebashi|ja|平和町 (前橋市)|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Yokosawa, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市横沢町)|12=draft}} – Located in Yokosawa, Maebashi. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Taguchi, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市田口町)|12=draft}} – Located in Taguchi, Maebashi. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Kiryū)|ja|雷電神社 (桐生市)|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Kiryū, Gunma|ja|桐生市|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Isezaki, Sakae-Iyoku)|ja|雷電神社 (伊勢崎市境伊与久)|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Sakae, Iyoku|ja|境伊与久|12=draft}}, {{ill|Isezaki, Gunma|ja|伊勢崎市|12=draft}}.

== Saitama Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Kaminomura Shrine|ja|上之村神社|12=draft}} (Daiden Shrine) – Located in {{ill|Kumagaya, Saitama|ja|熊谷市|12=draft}}, the shrine dates back 1,000 years and is associated with the {{ill|Raijin Shrine (Hyōgo)|ja|雷神社 (豊岡市)|12=draft}} in {{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|ja|豊岡市|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Satte)|ja|雷電神社 (幸手市)|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Satte, Saitama|ja|幸手市|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Kasukabe)|ja|雷電神社 (春日部市)|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Kasukabe, Saitama|ja|春日部市|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Saitama City)|ja|雷電神社 (さいたま市)|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Imawa, Kita-ku|ja|今羽町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Saitama City|ja|さいたま市|12=draft}}. * Raiden Shrine (Tokigawa) – Located in {{ill|Tokigawa, Saitama|ja|ときがわ町|12=draft}}, atop {{ill|Raiden Mountain (Saitama)|ja|雷電山 (埼玉県)|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Kazo)|ja|雷電神社 (加須市南大桑)|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市|12=draft}}.
== Tokyo ==

[[File:Itakura Raiden Shrine Honsha 1.JPG|thumb|220px|Raiden Shrine (群馬県板倉町)]] '''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a name shared by multiple [[Shinto shrine]]s located primarily in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. These shrines are commonly dedicated to deities associated with protection against [[thunder]], although their enshrined deities and origins vary.
Similar shrines include {{ill|Raijin Shrine|ja|雷神社|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Den Shrine|ja|電神社|12=draft|12=draft}}.
== Gunma Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)|ja|雷電神社 (板倉町)|12=draft|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Itakura, Gunma|ja|板倉町|12=draft|12=draft}}, [[Ōra District]], this shrine is also called Itakura Raiden Shrine. It holds an unofficial status as the head shrine of "Raiden Shrines" in the Kantō region. Enshrined deities include {{ill|Honoikazuchi-no-Ōkami|ja|火雷大神|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōikazuchi-no-Ōkami|ja|大雷大神|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Wakazuchi-no-Ōkami|ja|別雷大神|12=draft|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Minami, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市南町)|12=draft|12=draft}} – Located in [[Minami, Maebashi]] within {{ill|Maebashi, Gunma|ja|前橋市|12=draft|12=draft}}, behind the main hall of {{ill|Maebashi Shinmeigu|ja|前橋神明宮|12=draft|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Kamishinden, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市上新田町)|12=draft|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Kamishinden, Maebashi|ja|上新田町 (前橋市)|12=draft|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Heiwa, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市平和町)|12=draft|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Heiwa, Maebashi|ja|平和町 (前橋市)|12=draft|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Yokosawa, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市横沢町)|12=draft|12=draft}} – Located in Yokosawa, Maebashi. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Taguchi, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市田口町)|12=draft|12=draft}} – Located in Taguchi, Maebashi. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Kiryū)|ja|雷電神社 (桐生市)|12=draft|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Kiryū, Gunma|ja|桐生市|12=draft|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Isezaki, Sakae-Iyoku)|ja|雷電神社 (伊勢崎市境伊与久)|12=draft|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Sakae, Iyoku|ja|境伊与久|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Isezaki, Gunma|ja|伊勢崎市|12=draft|12=draft}}.
== Tochigi Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Hiraide, Utsunomiya)|ja|雷電神社 (宇都宮市平出町)|12=draft|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Hiraide, Utsunomiya|ja|平出町 (宇都宮市)|12=draft|12=draft}}, this shrine is also called Hiraide Raiden Shrine. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Wakazuchi-no-Ōkami|ja|別雷之大神|12=draft|12=draft}} and nine other deities. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Hosoya, Utsunomiya)|ja|雷電神社 (宇都宮市細谷町)|12=draft|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Hosoya, Utsunomiya|ja|細谷町 (宇都宮市)|12=draft|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Sukedo, Ashikaga)|ja|雷電神社 (足利市助戸東山町)|12=draft|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Sukedo, Ashikaga|ja|助戸東山町|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ashikaga, Tochigi|ja|足利市|12=draft|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Nogi, Tochigi)|ja|雷電神社 (野木町)|12=draft|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Nogi, Tochigi|ja|野木町|12=draft|12=draft}}, originally part of {{ill|Yanaka Village|ja|谷中村|12=draft|12=draft}}.
== Saitama Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Kaminomura Shrine|ja|上之村神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (Daiden Shrine) – Located in {{ill|Kumagaya, Saitama|ja|熊谷市|12=draft|12=draft}}, the shrine dates back 1,000 years and is associated with the {{ill|Raijin Shrine (Hyōgo)|ja|雷神社 (豊岡市)|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|ja|豊岡市|12=draft|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Satte)|ja|雷電神社 (幸手市)|12=draft|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Satte, Saitama|ja|幸手市|12=draft|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Kasukabe)|ja|雷電神社 (春日部市)|12=draft|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Kasukabe, Saitama|ja|春日部市|12=draft|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Saitama City)|ja|雷電神社 (さいたま市)|12=draft|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Imawa, Kita-ku|ja|今羽町|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Saitama City|ja|さいたま市|12=draft|12=draft}}. * Raiden Shrine (Tokigawa) – Located in {{ill|Tokigawa, Saitama|ja|ときがわ町|12=draft|12=draft}}, atop {{ill|Raiden Mountain (Saitama)|ja|雷電山 (埼玉県)|12=draft|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Kazo)|ja|雷電神社 (加須市南大桑)|12=draft|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市|12=draft|12=draft}}.

* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Shinjuku)|ja|雷電神社 (新宿区)|12=draft|12=draft}} – Located at {{ill|Hanazono Shrine|ja|花園神社|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Shinjuku, Tokyo|ja|新宿区|12=draft|12=draft}}. Merged with Hanazono Shrine in 1928 but still maintains its presence as {{ill|Raiden Inari Shrine|ja|雷電稲荷神社|12=draft|12=draft}}.
== Kanagawa Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Isehara)|ja|雷電神社 (伊勢原市)|12=draft|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Isehara, Kanagawa|ja|伊勢原市|12=draft|12=draft}}, affiliated with {{ill|Hibita Shrine|ja|比々多神社|12=draft|12=draft}}.
== Aichi Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Tahara)|ja|雷電神社 (田原市)|12=draft|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Tahara, Aichi|ja|田原市|12=draft|12=draft}}.
== Mie Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Ise)|ja|雷電神社 (伊勢市)|12=draft|12=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市|12=draft|12=draft}}. Considered a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, though later merged with the Hachioji Shrine.
== Related Articles ==

revision.comment: 52 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:雷電神社 up to rev 103660022
revision.text:
[[ファイル:上之雷電神社本殿（埼玉県指定文化財）.jpg|サムネイル|県指定文化財「上之雷電神社本殿」埼玉県熊谷市]] __NOTOC__ [[ファイル:Itakura Raiden Shrine Honsha 1.JPG|thumb|220px|雷電神社（群馬県板倉町）]] '''雷電神社'''（らいでんじんじゃ）は、｢雷電」を社名に持つ[[神社]]。関東地方に多い。一様に雷除けの神とされるが、祭神や由緒は必ずしも一定ではない。
類似の神社として、[[雷神社]]や[[電神社]]もある。
== 主な雷電神社 ==
=== 関東地方 === * [[雷電神社 (古河市雷電町)|雷電神社]] – [[茨城県]][[古河市]]雷電町 * [[雷電神社 (古河市東諸川)|雷電神社]] – 茨城県古河市東諸川 * [[雷電神社 (前橋市南町)|雷電神社]] – [[群馬県]][[前橋市]][[南町 (前橋市)|南町]] ： [[前橋神明宮]]の本殿裏側に鎮座。 * [[雷電神社 (前橋市上新田町)|雷電神社]] – 群馬県前橋市[[上新田町 (前橋市)|上新田町]] * [[雷電神社 (前橋市平和町)|雷電神社]] – 群馬県前橋市[[平和町 (前橋市)|平和町]] * [[雷電神社 (前橋市横沢町)|雷電神社]] – 群馬県前橋市[[横沢町 (前橋市)|横沢町]] * [[雷電神社 (前橋市田口町)|雷電神社]] – 群馬県前橋市[[田口町 (前橋市)|田口町]] * [[雷電神社 (桐生市)|雷電神社]] – 群馬県[[桐生市]][[錦町 (桐生市)|錦町]] * [[雷電神社 (伊勢崎市境伊与久)|雷電神社]] – 群馬県[[伊勢崎市]]境 * [[雷電神社 (板倉町)|雷電神社]] – 群馬県[[邑楽郡]][[板倉町]]板倉 ： 板倉雷電神社とも呼ばれる。旧社格は郷社。関東地方の「雷電神社」｢雷電社」の事実上の総本社格とされている。主な祭神は、火雷大神、大雷大神、別雷大神。 * [[雷電神社 (館林市上三林町)|雷電神社]] – 群馬県[[館林市]]上三林町
* [[平出雷電神社]] – [[栃木県]][[宇都宮市]]平出町 ： 祭神は別雷之大神をはじめとする十柱。 * [[雷電神社 (宇都宮市細谷町)|雷電神社]] – 栃木県宇都宮市細谷町 * [[雷電神社 (足利市助戸東山町)|雷電神社]] – 栃木県[[足利市]]助戸 * [[雷電神社 (野木町野木)|雷電神社]] – 栃木県[[下都賀郡]][[野木町]]野木 * [[雷電神社 (野木町友沼)|雷電神社]] – 栃木県下都賀郡野木町友沼
* [[雷電神社 (さいたま市)|雷電神社]] – [[埼玉県]][[さいたま市]][[北区 (さいたま市)|北区]][[今羽町]] * [[雷電神社 (熊谷市)|雷電神社]] – 埼玉県[[熊谷市]]御正新田[[ファイル:熊谷市最古の木製鳥居（熊谷市指定文化財）.jpg|サムネイル|市指定文化財「上之雷電神社両部鳥居」埼玉県熊谷市]][[ファイル:上之雷電神社本殿付扉一組（埼玉県指定文化財）.jpg|サムネイル|県指定文化財'''「'''雷電神社本殿付扉一組'''」'''永禄元年（1558）に成田長泰寄進による本殿御扉 埼玉県熊谷市]] *[[雷電神社 (加須市南大桑)|雷電神社]] – 埼玉県[[加須市]]南大桑 *[[雷電神社 (春日部市梅田)|雷電神社]] – 埼玉県[[春日部市]]梅田 *[[雷電神社 (春日部市下柳)|雷電神社]] – 埼玉県春日部市下柳 *[[雷電神社 (幸手市)|雷電神社]] – 埼玉県[[幸手市]][[中 (幸手市)|中]] *[[雷電神社 (ときがわ町)|雷電神社]] – 埼玉県[[比企郡]][[ときがわ町]]日影 ： [[雷電山 (埼玉県)|雷電山]]山頂 *[[雷電稲荷神社 (新宿区)|雷電稲荷神社]] – [[東京都]][[新宿区]][[新宿]] ： [[花園神社]]末社 * 雷電神社 – 東京都新宿区新宿 ： 昭和3年 (1928年) に花園神社と合祀され、現在は跡地。 * [[雷電神社 (伊勢原市)|雷電神社]] – [[神奈川県]][[伊勢原市]]串橋 ： [[比々多神社]]兼務社
=== 中部地方 === * [[雷電神社 (田原市)|雷電神社]] – [[愛知県]][[田原市]][[波瀬町]]
* [[雷電神社 (伊勢市)|雷電神社]] – [[三重県]][[伊勢市]]小俣町本町 ： [[延喜式神名帳#式内社|式内社]]と伝えられるが後に八王子社などと合祀されたものとされる。
== 関連項目 == * [[雷神社]] * [[電神社]] * [[若電神社]] * [[落雷]] *[[雷]] {{曖昧さ回避}} {{神道 横}}
{{デフォルトソート:らいてんしんしや}} [[Category:雷電神社|*]] [[Category:同名の神社]]

revision.text:
[[ファイル:上之雷電神社本殿（埼玉県指定文化財）.jpg|サムネイル|県指定文化財「上之雷電神社本殿」埼玉県熊谷市]] __NOTOC__ [[ファイル:Itakura Raiden Shrine Honsha 1.JPG|thumb|220px|雷電神社（群馬県板倉町）]] '''雷電神社'''（らいでんじんじゃ）は、｢雷電」を社名に持つ[[神社]]。関東地方に多い。一様に雷除けの神とされるが、祭神や由緒は必ずしも一定ではない。
類似の神社として、[[雷神社]]や[[電神社]]もある。
== 主な雷電神社 ==
=== 関東地方 === * [[雷電神社 (古河市雷電町)|雷電神社]] – [[茨城県]][[古河市]]雷電町 * [[雷電神社 (古河市東諸川)|雷電神社]] – 茨城県古河市東諸川 * [[雷電神社 (前橋市南町)|雷電神社]] – [[群馬県]][[前橋市]][[南町 (前橋市)|南町]] ： [[前橋神明宮]]の本殿裏側に鎮座。 * [[雷電神社 (前橋市上新田町)|雷電神社]] – 群馬県前橋市[[上新田町 (前橋市)|上新田町]] * [[雷電神社 (前橋市平和町)|雷電神社]] – 群馬県前橋市[[平和町 (前橋市)|平和町]] * [[雷電神社 (前橋市横沢町)|雷電神社]] – 群馬県前橋市[[横沢町 (前橋市)|横沢町]] * [[雷電神社 (前橋市田口町)|雷電神社]] – 群馬県前橋市[[田口町 (前橋市)|田口町]] * [[雷電神社 (桐生市)|雷電神社]] – 群馬県[[桐生市]][[錦町 (桐生市)|錦町]] * [[雷電神社 (伊勢崎市境伊与久)|雷電神社]] – 群馬県[[伊勢崎市]]境 * [[雷電神社 (板倉町)|雷電神社]] – 群馬県[[邑楽郡]][[板倉町]]板倉 ： 板倉雷電神社とも呼ばれる。旧社格は郷社。関東地方の「雷電神社」｢雷電社」の事実上の総本社格とされている。主な祭神は、火雷大神、大雷大神、別雷大神。 * [[雷電神社 (館林市上三林町)|雷電神社]] – 群馬県[[館林市]]上三林町
* [[平出雷電神社]] – [[栃木県]][[宇都宮市]]平出町 ： 祭神は別雷之大神をはじめとする十柱。 * [[雷電神社 (宇都宮市細谷町)|雷電神社]] – 栃木県宇都宮市細谷町 * [[雷電神社 (足利市助戸東山町)|雷電神社]] – 栃木県[[足利市]]助戸 * [[雷電神社 (野木町野木)|雷電神社]] – 栃木県[[下都賀郡]][[野木町]]野木 * [[雷電神社 (野木町友沼)|雷電神社]] – 栃木県下都賀郡野木町友沼
* [[雷電神社 (さいたま市)|雷電神社]] – [[埼玉県]][[さいたま市]][[北区 (さいたま市)|北区]][[今羽町]] * [[雷電神社 (熊谷市)|雷電神社]] – 埼玉県[[熊谷市]]御正新田[[ファイル:熊谷市最古の木製鳥居（熊谷市指定文化財）.jpg|サムネイル|市指定文化財「上之雷電神社両部鳥居」埼玉県熊谷市]][[ファイル:上之雷電神社本殿付扉一組（埼玉県指定文化財）.jpg|サムネイル|県指定文化財'''「'''雷電神社本殿付扉一組'''」'''永禄元年（1558）に成田長泰寄進による本殿御扉 埼玉県熊谷市]] *[[雷電神社 (加須市南大桑)|雷電神社]] – 埼玉県[[加須市]]南大桑 *[[雷電神社 (春日部市梅田)|雷電神社]] – 埼玉県[[春日部市]]梅田 *[[雷電神社 (春日部市下柳)|雷電神社]] – 埼玉県春日部市下柳 *[[雷電神社 (幸手市)|雷電神社]] – 埼玉県[[幸手市]][[中 (幸手市)|中]] *[[雷電神社 (ときがわ町)|雷電神社]] – 埼玉県[[比企郡]][[ときがわ町]]日影 ： [[雷電山 (埼玉県)|雷電山]]山頂 *[[雷電稲荷神社 (新宿区)|雷電稲荷神社]] – [[東京都]][[新宿区]][[新宿]] ： [[花園神社]]末社 * 雷電神社 – 東京都新宿区新宿 ： 昭和3年 (1928年) に花園神社と合祀され、現在は跡地。 * [[雷電神社 (伊勢原市)|雷電神社]] – [[神奈川県]][[伊勢原市]]串橋 ： [[比々多神社]]兼務社
=== 中部地方 === * [[雷電神社 (田原市)|雷電神社]] – [[愛知県]][[田原市]][[波瀬町]]
* [[雷電神社 (伊勢市)|雷電神社]] – [[三重県]][[伊勢市]]小俣町本町 ： [[延喜式神名帳#式内社|式内社]]と伝えられるが後に八王子社などと合祀されたものとされる。

[[File:Itakura Raiden Shrine Honsha 1.JPG|thumb|220px|Raiden Shrine (群馬県板倉町)]] '''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社) is a name shared by multiple [[Shinto shrine]]s located primarily in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. These shrines are commonly dedicated to deities associated with protection against [[thunder]], although their enshrined deities and origins vary.
Similar shrines include {{ill|Raijin Shrine|ja|雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Den Shrine|ja|電神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Gunma Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)|ja|雷電神社 (板倉町)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Itakura, Gunma|ja|板倉町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, [[Ōra District]], this shrine is also called Itakura Raiden Shrine. It holds an unofficial status as the head shrine of "Raiden Shrines" in the Kantō region. Enshrined deities include {{ill|Honoikazuchi-no-Ōkami|ja|火雷大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ōikazuchi-no-Ōkami|ja|大雷大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Wakazuchi-no-Ōkami|ja|別雷大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Minami, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市南町)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in [[Minami, Maebashi]] within {{ill|Maebashi, Gunma|ja|前橋市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, behind the main hall of {{ill|Maebashi Shinmeigu|ja|前橋神明宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Kamishinden, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市上新田町)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Kamishinden, Maebashi|ja|上新田町 (前橋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Heiwa, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市平和町)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Heiwa, Maebashi|ja|平和町 (前橋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Yokosawa, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市横沢町)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in Yokosawa, Maebashi. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Taguchi, Maebashi)|ja|雷電神社 (前橋市田口町)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in Taguchi, Maebashi. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Kiryū)|ja|雷電神社 (桐生市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Kiryū, Gunma|ja|桐生市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Isezaki, Sakae-Iyoku)|ja|雷電神社 (伊勢崎市境伊与久)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Sakae, Iyoku|ja|境伊与久|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Isezaki, Gunma|ja|伊勢崎市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Tochigi Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Hiraide, Utsunomiya)|ja|雷電神社 (宇都宮市平出町)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Hiraide, Utsunomiya|ja|平出町 (宇都宮市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, this shrine is also called Hiraide Raiden Shrine. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Wakazuchi-no-Ōkami|ja|別雷之大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and nine other deities. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Hosoya, Utsunomiya)|ja|雷電神社 (宇都宮市細谷町)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Hosoya, Utsunomiya|ja|細谷町 (宇都宮市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Sukedo, Ashikaga)|ja|雷電神社 (足利市助戸東山町)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Sukedo, Ashikaga|ja|助戸東山町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ashikaga, Tochigi|ja|足利市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Nogi, Tochigi)|ja|雷電神社 (野木町)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Nogi, Tochigi|ja|野木町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, originally part of {{ill|Yanaka Village|ja|谷中村|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Saitama Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Kaminomura Shrine|ja|上之村神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Daiden Shrine) – Located in {{ill|Kumagaya, Saitama|ja|熊谷市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine dates back 1,000 years and is associated with the {{ill|Raijin Shrine (Hyōgo)|ja|雷神社 (豊岡市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|ja|豊岡市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Satte)|ja|雷電神社 (幸手市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Satte, Saitama|ja|幸手市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Kasukabe)|ja|雷電神社 (春日部市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Kasukabe, Saitama|ja|春日部市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Saitama City)|ja|雷電神社 (さいたま市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Imawa, Kita-ku|ja|今羽町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Saitama City|ja|さいたま市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * Raiden Shrine (Tokigawa) – Located in {{ill|Tokigawa, Saitama|ja|ときがわ町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, atop {{ill|Raiden Mountain (Saitama)|ja|雷電山 (埼玉県)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Kazo)|ja|雷電神社 (加須市南大桑)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Tokyo ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Shinjuku)|ja|雷電神社 (新宿区)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located at {{ill|Hanazono Shrine|ja|花園神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Shinjuku, Tokyo|ja|新宿区|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Merged with Hanazono Shrine in 1928 but still maintains its presence as {{ill|Raiden Inari Shrine|ja|雷電稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Kanagawa Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Isehara)|ja|雷電神社 (伊勢原市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Isehara, Kanagawa|ja|伊勢原市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, affiliated with {{ill|Hibita Shrine|ja|比々多神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Aichi Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Tahara)|ja|雷電神社 (田原市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Tahara, Aichi|ja|田原市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Mie Prefecture ==
* {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Ise)|ja|雷電神社 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Considered a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, though later merged with the Hachioji Shrine.
== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|Raijin Shrine|ja|雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Den Shrine|ja|電神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Wakaden Shrine|ja|若電神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Lightning|ja|落雷|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Thunder|ja|雷|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{draft categories|
}}
